cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about garage sales and what we know from the passage. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go back to the passage and read the part about garage sales. Let me skim through the text again. The passage says that weekend outdoor garage sales have now become very much a part of American culture. They're held usually in the spring and fall when the weather is still good. The homeowner decides to clear out items that are no longer useful and advertises in a local paper. The goods on display are in good condition and great buys can be found with time and patience. The owner places items along the driveway or front lawn. Examples of items include old magazines, books, etc. Also, if the owner is selling their home or moving soon, they might give things away at prices far below their true worth. Now looking at the options: A) It is only held in the spring of the year. But the passage says "usually in the spring and fall". So "only" is incorrect. So A is wrong. B) The goods on display are totally useless. The passage says the homeowner is clearing out items that are "no longer useful to him", but the goods are in good condition and can be great buys. So "totally useless" is not correct. B is wrong. C) Garage sales are popular with Americans. The passage states they've become very much a part of American culture.
